---
title: Release Templates
description: How to create and use templates to standardize your release workflow.
---

Templates let you save a reusable set of tasks and marketing activities that can be applied to any new release.

:::note
Templates require a **Team** or **Label** plan.
:::

## Why use templates

If you follow a similar process for every release (e.g., submit to distributors, pitch playlists, schedule social posts), templates let you set it up once and reuse it.

## Creating a template

1. Go to the **Templates** page
2. Click **New Template**
3. Give it a name (e.g., "Standard Single Rollout")
4. Add tasks and marketing activities

### Adding tasks to a template

Each template task includes:

- Title
- Description (optional)
- Relative timing (e.g., "2 weeks before release")

### Adding marketing activities to a template

Each template marketing activity includes:

- Title
- Platform
- Phase (Pre-save, Release Week, Post-Release)

## Applying a template to a release

1. Open a release and go to the **Tasks** tab
2. Click **Import from Template**
3. Select the template you want to apply
4. All tasks and marketing activities from the template are added to the release

You can still edit, add, or remove individual items after importing.

## Managing templates

- **Edit** -- update the template name, tasks, or activities
- **Duplicate** -- create a copy of an existing template to use as a starting point
- **Delete** -- remove a template you no longer need

## Built-in templates

ReleaseLoop includes a **Standard Single Rollout** template as a starting point. You can duplicate and customize it to match your workflow.
